title: Follow a Recipe
summary: Read a recipe all the way through, understand the steps, and cook a dish from start to finish.
learnerPromise: Read and follow a recipe from start to finish without getting lost.
whyItMatters: Recipes are roadmaps for cooking. Learning to read them carefully helps you understand timing, avoid mistakes, and feel more confident trying new dishes.
realLifeUses: Cook a new dish you have never tried before
Understand cooking terms like simmer, saute, and fold
Estimate how long a meal will take to prepare
Scale a recipe up or down for more or fewer servings
youWillLearn: How to read through a full recipe before starting
What mise en place means and why it helps
How to adjust timing based on what you see and smell
miniChallenge: Pick one simple recipe and make it, following each step in order.
steps: Read the entire recipe from start to finish before touching any ingredients.
Gather every ingredient and tool listed — this is called mise en place.
Measure and prep each ingredient so it is ready to use when the recipe calls for it.
Follow the steps in order, paying attention to words like simmer, saute, and fold.
Taste as you go if it is safe to do so, and adjust seasoning near the end.
completionCriteria: The dish is prepared in the correct order
All listed ingredients were used appropriately
commonProblems: Skipped reading ahead and got surprised by a long marinating or chilling step
Misread a measurement — double-check teaspoons versus tablespoons
Burned one component while prepping another — mise en place prevents this
tips: Start with recipes labeled easy or beginner
Read the notes section of a recipe for helpful substitutions
Set out a trash bowl next to your cutting board to keep your workspace clean
